title: Colorimetric Iron Quantification Assay
author: Natalie Rubio
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x2ufqew

steps.instruction: 1. Equilibrate the iron standard, iron assay buffer and iron reducer to room temperature. 2. Thaw the iron probe and then keep on ice during the duration of the assay. 01:00:00

1. Dilute 10 μL of iron standard in 990 μL of distilled water to prepare a 1 mM standard. Prepare a standard curve dilution in a 96-well plate. %s %s 2. Set up the dilution for a duplicate reading (2 x 100 μL).

steps.title: Use fresh samples or thaw snap frozen samples on ice
steps.instruction: Use fresh samples or thaw snap frozen samples on ice. 02:00:00

steps.title: Weigh 10 mg of tissue sample for each condition
steps.instruction: Weigh 10 mg of tissue sample for each condition.

steps.title: Wash the tissue in cold PBS
steps.instruction: Wash the tissue in cold PBS.

steps.title: Homogenize each tissue in 300 μL...
steps.instruction: Homogenize each tissue in 300 μL iron assay buffer with a Dounce homogenizer on ice with 15 passes.

steps.title: Transfer the homogenates from the homogenizer to microcentrifuge tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenates from the homogenizer to microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge the homogenates at 16,000 xg...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the homogenates at 16,000 xg for 10 minutes to remove insoluble particles. 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer the supernatants to clean microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatants to clean microcentrifuge tubes and keep on ice.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of standard dilutions...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of standard dilutions in duplicate to a 96-well plate.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of each sample...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of each sample in quadruplicate (duplicate for Fe 2+ , duplicate for total Fe) to the 96-well plate and add 50 μL iron assay buffer to each sample to bring the total volume to 100 μL per well.

steps.title: Add 5 μL of iron reducer to each standard well
steps.instruction: Add 5 μL of iron reducer to each standard well.

steps.title: For Fe 2+ measurement, add 5...
steps.instruction: For Fe 2+ measurement, add 5 μL of assay buffer to each sample.

steps.title: For total Fe measurement, add 5...
steps.instruction: For total Fe measurement, add 5 μL of iron reducer to each sample.

steps.title: Mix and incubate standards and samples...
steps.instruction: Mix and incubate standards and samples at 25°C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00

steps.title: Add 100 μL iron probe to...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL iron probe to each well containing the iron standard and test samples.

steps.title: Mix and incubate at 25°C for...
steps.instruction: Mix and incubate at 25°C for 60 minutes, in the dark. 01:00:00

steps.title: Measure output on a colorimetric microplate reader at OD 593
steps.instruction: Measure output on a colorimetric microplate reader at OD 593.

steps.title: Average the duplicate reader for each standard and sample
steps.instruction: Average the duplicate reader for each standard and sample.

steps.title: Substrate the mean absorbance value of the blank (standard no
steps.instruction: Substrate the mean absorbance value of the blank (standard no.

steps.title: from all standard and sample readings...
steps.instruction: from all standard and sample readings to obtain the corrected absorbance.

steps.title: Plot the corrected absorbance values for...
steps.instruction: Plot the corrected absorbance values for each standard as a function of the final iron concentration.

steps.title: Calculate the line of best fit
steps.instruction: Calculate the line of best fit. y = 0.0826x + 0.0199, R 2 = 0.9987

steps.title: Determine Fe 2+ and total Fe from the standard curve
steps.instruction: Determine Fe 2+ and total Fe from the standard curve.

steps.title: Calculate Fe 3+ by subtracting Fe...
steps.instruction: Calculate Fe 3+ by subtracting Fe 2+ from the total Fe.

steps.title: Iron concentration is calculated as
steps.instruction: Iron concentration is calculated as: [C] = (Sa/Sv)*D [C] = iron concentration Sa = content of iron in well calculated from standard curve (nmol) Sv = volume of sample added into the reaction wells (μL) D = sample dilution factor (if necessary) (The molecular weight of iron is 55.845 g/mol.)
